reasoning: In 2025, Lewis Hamilton will join Charles Leclerc at Ferrari. Assessing their head-to-head matchup, Charles Leclerc has significant advantages: he is deeply embedded within the Ferrari team, is currently in his prime at 27 years old, and is widely considered one of the fastest qualifiers on the grid, which offers crucial track position. While Hamilton is a seven-time World Champion offering exceptional race pace and tire management, he will be 40 years old at the start of the season and will need to adapt to a new car, a different engineering team, and a new team culture. Hamilton's recent qualifying performances against teammate George Russell also suggest slightly declining single-lap pace. The base rate of established elite drivers beating newly arriving older champions is relatively high (e.g., Leclerc vs. Vettel in 2019/2020). Therefore, Leclerc is the favored driver to score more points, with Hamilton's probability estimated at roughly 35%.
